content:
Employee Creation

Purpose

This guide explains how to create an Employee record in the system and the minimum information required to make the employee usable for HR operations (leave, payroll, attendance, approvals, and reporting).

Who Can Create Employees

Only users with the correct HR permissions should create Employee records, typically: - HR Manager - HR User (with create/write rights on Employee) - System Manager (for setup and support)

Preconditions

Before creating an employee, confirm the following records already exist: - Company - Department - Designation - Branch (if used) - Reports To manager (if known) - Employment Type / Contract Type (if configured) - User account (if the employee needs system login)

Navigation

  1. Open Home.

  2. Search for Employee.

  3. Click New.

Employee Creation Steps

  1. In the Employee form, complete identity fields:

  • First Name

  • Middle Name (optional)

  • Last Name

  • Gender

  • Date of Birth

  1. Complete employment details:

  • Company

  • Employee Number (if manual numbering is enabled)

  • Date of Joining

  • Department

  • Designation

  • Employment Type

  • Status (set to Active for current staff)

  1. Complete organization and reporting details:

  • Branch (if applicable)

  • Reports To

  • Grade (if applicable)

  1. Complete contact details:

  • Prefered Email / Company Email

  • Personal Email (optional)

  • Cell Number

  1. Complete payroll and HR control fields where applicable:

  • Holiday List

  • Default Shift

  • Leave Approver

  • Expense Approver

  • Attendance Device ID (if biometric integration exists)

  1. If the employee requires portal/system access:

  • Link a User ID

  • Ensure role assignment is handled by HR/IT policy

  1. Click Save.

Recommended Post-Creation Actions

After saving the employee record: 1. Create or verify user account and role profile. 2. Create employment contract (if your process requires it). 3. Assign leave policy / leave allocation. 4. Confirm payroll inclusion and salary structure assignment. 5. Confirm reporting line and approvers.

Validation Checklist

Use this checklist before considering setup complete: - Employee status is Active. - Department and designation are correct. - Date of joining is correct. - Employee is linked to the correct company. - Approvers are set (leave/expense where required). - Contact and email details are valid. - User ID is linked (if login is required).

Common Errors and Fixes

  • Missing mandatory fields: Complete highlighted mandatory fields then save again.

  • Invalid company/department/designation: Create the missing master records first.

  • No naming series/employee number issue: Confirm Employee naming settings and series permissions.

  • Approver not selectable: Ensure the approver employee/user is active and has required permissions.

  • User link not allowed: Verify the email/user exists and is not linked to another employee incorrectly.

Data Quality Rules

  • Use official names exactly as in legal/HR documents.

  • Use company email as primary where policy requires.

  • Do not duplicate employee records for the same person.

  • Keep status updated (Active, Left, Suspended) for accurate reporting.

Audit and Control

  • Employee creation should follow HR onboarding approval.

  • Changes to key fields (department, designation, reports to, status) should be traceable.

  • Periodically review active employee list for data accuracy.

Related Documents

  • Employee Onboarding Process

  • Contract Management Guide

  • Leave Allocation Guide

  • Payroll Setup Guide

Revision History

  • 2026-05-06: Initial version created.

title: Home - Employee Creation

content:
Performance Appraisals

Purpose

This guide explains how HR runs performance appraisals end to end: building a reusable template, creating an appraisal cycle for many staff at once, the supervisor review and automatic grading, and how progress and results appear on the HR Management dashboard.

Key Concepts

  • Performance Appraisal Template – the reusable definition of an appraisal: performance areas & criteria, the rating scale, score bands, and the supervisor-comment questions. One template is marked Active and is used by default.
  • Performance Appraisal Cycle – an HR batch covering one evaluation period. HR selects employees once and submits; the system creates one appraisal per employee.
  • Performance Appraisal – the individual record completed by a supervisor for one employee.

1. Set up a template (once)

Go to Performance Appraisal Template and create a new record. On a new template the following are prefilled with sensible defaults that you can edit:

  • Performance Areas – each area with its criteria (e.g. Job Knowledge and Skills, Quality of Work).
  • Rating Scale – pulled from the Performance Appraisal Rating scale reference (1–5 descriptions).
  • Score Bands – the percentage bands that turn a score into a rating (Exceptional, Strong Performer, … Unacceptable) and the recommended action for each.
  • Supervisor Comments – the open-ended questions the supervisor answers (Highlights, Development Needs, etc.).

Tick Active and save. Only one template can be active at a time; activating a new one deactivates the previous. Update questions here at any time – future appraisals pick up the change.

2. Create an appraisal cycle

From the HR Management dashboard open the Performance Appraisal tab and click + New Appraisal Cycle (or create a Performance Appraisal Cycle directly). Set:

  • Evaluation Type and the From / To dates of the period.
  • Appraisal Template – leave blank to use the active template.
  • Employees – add every staff member who should be appraised this cycle.

3. Submit the cycle

Submitting the cycle creates one Performance Appraisal per employee, seeded from the template, and emails each employee’s supervisor (the employee is CC’d) asking them to complete it. Employees with no supervisor (reports_to) are skipped and flagged on the cycle. You can do this for many employees in a single action.

4. Supervisor completes the appraisal

The supervisor opens the appraisal, rates each performance area, answers the comment questions, and submits it back to HR. Email notifications are sent on every workflow transition (to supervisor, to HR, on approval, rejection, or request for clarification).

5. Automatic grading

When the supervisor submits to HR, the appraisal is graded automatically:

  • Total = sum of the area ratings (max 5 per area).
  • Score (%) = total ÷ (number of areas × 5).
  • Performance Rating = the highest score band the percentage meets, with its Recommended Action.

These fields appear in the Performance Score section of the appraisal. Because the maximum scales with the number of performance areas, grading stays correct even if the template changes.

6. HR review

HR approves, rejects, or requests clarification. A cycle is Completed only when every appraisal in it reaches Approved by HR; otherwise it shows as In Progress.

Dashboard

The Performance Appraisal tab on the HR Management dashboard shows:

  • A toggle to list only incomplete cycles, with per-cycle Total / Completed / Pending counts and status.
  • Charts of graded appraisals: Performance Rating Distribution and Average Score by Department, plus headline metrics.

Roles

  • HR Manager creates templates and cycles, submits cycles, and approves/rejects appraisals.
  • Supervisor completes and submits the appraisals for their direct reports.
meta_description: How HR runs performance appraisals: templates, cycles, supervisor review, grading, and the dashboard.
meta_keywords: performance appraisal, template, cycle, grading, HR
name: perfappraisaldoc1
title: Performance Appraisals
